questions.text: What is the 'Reminder' (Dhikr) mentioned in the first verse?
questions.answer: The Quran.

questions.text: What did the disbelievers find strange about the Prophet's message of Tawhid (Verse 5)?
questions.answer: They asked 'Has he made the gods [into] one God? Indeed, this is a curious thing'.

questions.text: Describe the dispute that came to Prophet Dawud (AS) in his prayer chamber.
questions.answer: Two litigants climbed over the wall; one had 99 ewes and the other had only one, but the first demanded the one ewe for himself.

questions.text: What was Dawud's (AS) realization after judging the dispute?
questions.answer: He realized it was a test for him from Allah, so he fell down in prostration and repented.

questions.text: What was the 'trial' of Sulayman (AS) mentioned in Verse 34?
questions.answer: Allah placed a [lifeless] body on his throne, after which he turned back to Allah in repentance.

questions.text: What was Sulayman's (AS) famous prayer for a kingdom?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, forgive me and grant me a kingdom such as will not belong to anyone after me'.

questions.text: Who were the 'Shayatin' (devils) subjected to Sulayman?
questions.answer: Every builder and diver, and others bound together in shackles.

questions.text: Which Prophet is the example of 'patience' in this Surah?
questions.answer: Prophet Ayyub (Job).

questions.text: How was Ayyub (AS) told to heal himself?
questions.answer: 'Strike [the ground] with your foot; this is a [spring for] a cool bath and drink'.

questions.text: What was the 'oath' Ayyub (AS) had to fulfill using a bundle of grass?
questions.answer: He had sworn to strike his wife (due to a misunderstanding during his illness), so Allah showed him a way to fulfill his oath without harming her.

questions.text: Who are the three Prophets mentioned as 'possessors of strength and vision' (Verse 45)?
questions.answer: Ibrahim, Ishaq, and Ya'qub.

questions.text: What is the description of the 'Abodes of the Righteous'?
questions.answer: Gardens of perpetual residence, with doors opened for them, reclining therein and calling for abundant fruit and drink.

questions.text: What will the people of Hell ask about those they thought were 'evil' in the world?
questions.answer: They will wonder why they do not see the men they used to count among the bad (the believers).

questions.text: Describe the scene of Adam's creation as narrated at the end of the Surah.
questions.answer: Allah informed the angels He would create a human from clay, and when fashioned, they must prostrate.

questions.text: What was Iblis's specific excuse for not prostrating to Adam?
questions.answer: 'I am better than him. You created me from fire and created him from clay'.

questions.text: What is the 'Curse' upon Iblis until the Day of Recompense?
questions.answer: He is expelled and accursed.

questions.text: How did Iblis vow to mislead mankind?
questions.answer: By Allah's might, he vowed to mislead them all, except the 'Mukhlaseen' (sincere servants).

questions.text: What is the Prophet's (SAW) declaration regarding his mission in Verse 86?
questions.answer: 'I do not ask you for it any payment, and I am not of the pretenders'.

questions.text: What is the 'great news' (Naba'un Azim) mentioned in Verse 67?
questions.answer: The Quran and the news of the Hereafter, which the people are turning away from.

questions.text: What did Allah say about His purpose in creating the heavens and earth?
questions.answer: He did not create them 'in aimless play' (Batila); that is the assumption of those who disbelieve.
